hero.title: Chris Hemmings
hero.lead:
Helping men understand themselves, communicate more honestly and build more meaningful lives.

BACP-registered | Founder of Men's Therapy Hub | Co-host of No Man's an Island

about.title: A little about me
about.body:
I work as a therapist, coach and public speaker, helping men better understand their mental health, improve communication and build stronger relationships. Whether I'm working one-to-one in the therapy room or speaking to audiences, my aim is always the same: to create honest conversations that lead to meaningful change.

Alongside my private practice, I founded [**Men's Therapy Hub**](https://menstherapyhub.co.uk) and [**M-Path**](https://m-path.co.uk), two organisations dedicated to improving men's mental health and emotional wellbeing. Through this work, I support more men and boys to access help, challenge unhelpful ideas about themselves and engage more openly with their emotional wellbeing.

Before retraining as a therapist, I spent over a decade as a BBC journalist and documentary maker, telling human stories and exploring the issues that shape our lives. Today, I bring that experience together with therapeutic insight through therapy, public speaking, workshops and advocacy across the UK and internationally.

I'm also the author of _Be a Man_, exploring the lasting impact of traditional ideas of masculinity on men and boys. Everything I do is driven by a belief that better conversations help people better understand themselves and those around them.

featuredIn.title: My work has been featured in

interests.title: Areas of specific interest
interests.intro: I have experience working with clients with a wide range of challenges, including:

items.title: Relationships
items.body: From conflict to communication issues, we'll explore how you relate to others — and to yourself — to create connection in your life.

items.title: Anger
items.body: Anger often hides pain, shame or fear. Together we'll unpack where it comes from and how to express it in healthier ways.

items.title: Identity
items.body: If you are questioning what kind of man you want to be, or feeling stuck between expectations and your true self.

items.title: Substance abuse
items.body: Whatever the substance you're misusing, we'll work to understand what's driving the cycle and how to break it with compassion.

items.title: Low self-esteem
items.body: If your inner critic is loud or constant, therapy can help you challenge unhelpful beliefs and build a stronger sense of self.

items.title: Depression
items.body: When life feels heavy or flat, we can create a space for you to reconnect with yourself, your emotions and what brings meaning.

items.title: Anxiety
items.body: Exploring the roots of worry, overthinking and tension — and finding ways to feel calmer, more grounded and in control.

items.title: Emotional expression
items.body: If you've grown up being told to hide how you feel, we can work to unlearn that and reconnect you with your full emotional range.

speakingPromo.title: Bring this conversation to your workplace
speakingPromo.body: I'm also a keynote speaker and workshop facilitator on men's mental health, masculinity and emotional wellbeing — delivering workplace workshops and talks for employers, schools, universities and conferences.

seo.title: Chris Hemmings | Therapist, Counsellor & Coach for Men
seo.description: Chris Hemmings is a BACP-registered therapist, coach and founder of Men's Therapy Hub. Online therapy and coaching for men in the UK and worldwide. Free 15-minute consultation.

og.title: Chris Hemmings
og.subtitle: BACP-registered therapist & coach helping men build healthier relationships, greater confidence and emotional wellbeing
